mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) during rate-cutting periods, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations and triggers negative equity repricing within one month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE crosses above 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in real GDP growth and acceleration in consumer price inflation creates stagflationary expectations that force equity valuation multiples to contract over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with an out-of-sample accuracy above 50 percent, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity constraints and slowing output that directly suppresses corporate earnings forecasts and equity prices.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in over 60 percent of cases, invalidating the supply-constraint pricing mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates crossing above 3.0 percent increase the cost of capital and trigger immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities, producing negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 58 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0 percent, or the signal shows zero predictive power in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand that supports consumer revenue projections and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows, or fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales figures demonstrate strong consumer expenditure that directly translates into higher corporate top-line growth and generates positive equity market momentum over one week.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or the series shows no significant autocorrelation with short-term market direction.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a monetary tightening cycle signal reduced construction activity and lower capital expenditure expectations that depress equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 60-day returns in over 60 percent of instances, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is rising.
